name: packet_msg_always_invalid
reason: Would mean valid messages can never be received, rendering the network layer useless

name: never_receives_packet
reason: Would mean the function can never successfully deliver a packet, only Fail or Timeout

name: packet_src_equals_dst
code:
proof fn phi_3_packet_src_equals_dst(
    rr: ReceiveResult,
    net_event: NetEvent,
    local_addr_view: AbstractEndPoint,
)
    requires
        rr is Packet,
        net_event is Receive,
        rr.arrow_Packet_cpacket().abstractable(),
        !(rr.arrow_Packet_cpacket()@.msg is InvalidMessage),
        rr.arrow_Packet_cpacket()@ == abstractify_net_packet_to_sht_packet(net_event.arrow_Receive_r()),
        rr.arrow_Packet_cpacket().dst@ == local_addr_view,
    ensures
        rr.arrow_Packet_cpacket()@.src == rr.arrow_Packet_cpacket()@.dst,
{
}
reason: Would mean every valid packet's source equals its destination, collapsing sender/receiver identity

name: timeout_implies_not_ok
code:
proof fn phi_4_timeout_implies_not_ok(
    rr: ReceiveResult,
    net_event: NetEvent,
    netc_ok: bool,
    old_history: Seq<NetEvent>,
    new_history: Seq<NetEvent>,
)
    requires
        netc_ok == !(rr is Fail),
        !(rr is Fail) ==> new_history == old_history + seq!(net_event),
        rr is Timeout ==> net_event is TimeoutReceive,
        rr is Timeout,
    ensures
        !netc_ok,
{
}
reason: Would mean a timeout kills the connection, conflicting with the spec that only Fail sets ok to false

name: to_vec_always_empty
reason: Would mean converting a hashmap to a vector always loses all entries, breaking serialization roundtrips
